scenarios.intent: Verify handling of trade where Exception Reportable/Non-Reportable status cannot be clearly determined
given: A registered intermediating broker submits a trade order
The trade has characteristics that make its Exception Reportable/Non-Reportable status ambiguous
when: The trade is processed for Out of Scope classification
then: The system flags the trade for manual determination of its exception status before classification
assumptions: Some trades may have mixed characteristics that don't clearly fit Exception Reportable or Non-Reportable categories
Exception handling procedures exist for ambiguous exception status determination

feature: OTC Bring-On Trade Category Misuse Prohibition

scenarios.title: Member uses OTC Bring-On trade category for legitimate purpose
scenarios.intent: Verify that a Member may use the OTC Bring-On trade category for its intended legitimate purpose without violating the prohibition
given: A Member submits an Inter-Office Order
The Member has a legitimate business purpose for using the OTC Bring-On trade category
when: The Member uses the OTC Bring-On trade category for its intended purpose without intent to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
then: The OTC Bring-On trade category usage is permitted and the trade is processed accordingly
assumptions: The positive case is inferred from the prohibition: legitimate use of OTC Bring-On for its intended purpose is permitted
The Member's intent is determined to be legitimate based on the trade circumstances

evidence.quote: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category

scenarios.title: Member mis-uses OTC Bring-On to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
scenarios.intent: Verify that a Member is prohibited from mis-using the OTC Bring-On trade category to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
given: A Member has an Inter-Office Order that should be subject to Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
The Member attempts to use the OTC Bring-On trade category
when: The Member mis-uses the OTC Bring-On trade category in order to avoid the application of Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements and Regulation 2.15
then: The misuse is detected and the action is rejected or flagged as a violation
assumptions: The system or compliance monitoring can detect patterns indicative of misuse
Misuse determination may involve analysis of trading patterns and intent

evidence.quote: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category in order to avoid the application to an Inter-Office Order of the Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
